2026-04-17T10:35:23.9545935Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9546904Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9547688Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9549671Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9550295Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9550729Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9551028Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9551370Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9551676Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9552154Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9552527Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9553065Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T10:35:23.9553545Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-17T10:38:52.5157607Z **Summary of gaps found in PR #58274:**
2026-04-17T10:38:52.5157992Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:53.5748934Z - `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` README — `Logging` section is a single sentence; doesn't document the new auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` (log levels, fields, query-string exclusion). Also missing a Key concepts entry for it.
2026-04-17T10:38:54.5071643Z - `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` README — `Logging` section doesn't cover the new handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`; no explanation of the new deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation logic.

2026-04-17T10:38:58.4246480Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4247350Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net && cat sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/CHANGELOG.md
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4248169Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4248985Z ◆ Now I have a clearer picture. The commit introduces two main new features that need documentation:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4249795Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4250097Z   1. **In the Core package (beta.22)**:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4252815Z      - New inbound request logging middleware (`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`) that is now auto-enabled. The README's Logging section only mentions "Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues" but doesn't document this new middleware, what it logs, or how to configure log levels.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4255428Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4255775Z   2. **In the Responses package (beta.2)**:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4256454Z      - New deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation using SHA-256 hash
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4257229Z      - Handler-level diagnostic logging across all endpoints
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4257855Z      - New `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4258308Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4261299Z   The critical gap is that the Core README's Logging section doesn't explain what the new middleware actually logs, how to configure log levels, or what gets excluded—and the Responses package documentation similarly lacks details on the structured log format, the `agent_session_id` derivation mechanism, and how to configure the new logging features.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4263829Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4270436Z   Since the Core package has auto-enabled middleware that affects all users, I should prioritize documenting what's being logged automatically, what security measures are in place (like query string exclusion), and what the structured log entries look like. The Responses package gaps around session ID derivation and handler-level logging are secondary but still worth addressing. I'm weighing which documentation gap to prioritize—the Core package's auto-enabled logging middleware that affects all users versus the Responses package's new session ID behavior. The middleware seems more critical since it's foundational and the security implications around query string exclusion aren't documented at all. Let me check the middleware's public API to understand what needs to be documented.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4275223Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4276000Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net && cat sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/src/AgentHostMiddlewareExtensions.cs
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4276602Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4277340Z ✓ $ cd /home/runner/work/azure-sdk-for-net/azure-sdk-for-net && grep -n "InboundRequest\|logging\|LogRequest" sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/src/AgentHostBuilder.cs | head -30
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4278026Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4278216Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture of the documentation gaps:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4278513Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4279923Z   1. **Core README - Logging section**: The inbound request logging middleware is automatically registered when using `AgentHostBuilder`, but the README's Logging section doesn't document this at all. It only says "Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues." Users need to know:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4281163Z      - What logging happens automatically
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4281524Z      - Log levels (Information for start/success, Warning for 4xx/5xx)
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4282042Z      - What's included (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers, trace ID)
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4282535Z      - What's excluded (query strings, for security reasons)
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4282854Z      - How to configure log verbosity
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4283090Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4283705Z   2. **Responses README - Logging section**: The new handler-level structured logging and `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` are new in beta.2 but not documented in the README.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4284382Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4284986Z   3. **Responses README - Missing `agent_session_id` concept**: The deterministic session ID derivation is a significant new feature but is not explained anywhere in the README.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4285662Z   
2026-04-17T10:38:58.4286602Z   The Core README has the most impactful gap since the InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware is auto-enabled for all users, has security implications around query string exclusion, and the Logging section is just a single sentence. I should start by filing an issue for this.

Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-17T10:38:58.6270692Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T10:38:43.371205264Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary)\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/`\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by @RaviPidaparthi\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs:\n\n1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\n\n2. **Responses beta.2** — Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation via SHA-256 of `\"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\"` (63-char lowercase hex); structured `Information`-level logs added to all endpoint handlers; new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage call.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` is a single sentence that says \"Enable ASP.NET Core logging to diagnose startup issues\" — it does not mention the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, what it logs, at which levels, or that query strings are excluded.\n- The `Key concepts` section of the Core README has no entry for the inbound request logging behaviour.\n- The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` does not document the new handler-level structured logs, the `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, or how to surface them.\n- The Responses README has no explanation of the `agent_session_id` field or how its value is determined (deterministic hash vs. random fallback).\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File 1 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`\n\n**Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~82) and replace it with an expanded description:\n\n```markdown\n### Logging\n\n`AgentHostBuilder` automatically enables structured HTTP request logging via `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. For every inbound request the middleware emits structured log entries under the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` category:\n\n| Event | Level | Fields |\n|-------|-------|--------|\n| Request started | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `xRequestId`, `clientRequestId`, `traceId` |\n| Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |\n| Request completed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |\n\n\u003e **Security note:** Query strings are never logged to avoid capturing sensitive data.\n\nTo enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"Logging\": {\n    \"LogLevel\": {\n      \"Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core\": \"Information\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources.\n```\n\n**Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry:\n\n```markdown\n### Inbound request logging\n\n`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs.\n```\n\n#### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`\n\n**Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder:\n\n```markdown\n### Logging\n\nThe library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Log category: `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Internal.ResponseEndpointHandler`.\n\nKey log events per endpoint:\n\n- **POST /responses (create):** response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, streaming flag, background flag, store flag, model.\n- **POST /responses (non-streaming completion):** final status and output item count.\n- **GET /responses/{id}:** response ID, retrieved status, output item count.\n- **POST /responses/{id}/cancel:** response ID and result status.\n- **DELETE /responses/{id}:** response ID and success flag.\n- **GET /responses/{id}/input_items:** response ID.\n\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\n\nEnable these logs in `appsettings.json`:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"Logging\": {\n    \"LogLevel\": {\n      \"Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses\": \"Information\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\nThe library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\n```\n\n**Also add** a `### Session ID` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Thread safety` entry:\n\n```markdown\n### Session ID (`agent_session_id`)\n\nEach response carries an `agent_session_id` that groups related responses into a conversation session. The value is resolved in priority order:\n\n1. Explicit value from the request payload.\n2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable.\n3. **Deterministic derivation** (default): SHA-256 of `\"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is the conversation ID (preferred) or the partition key extracted from `previous_response_id`.\n4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available.\n\nThis matches the cross-language session ID contract so sessions are consistent whether the client uses the .NET or Python SDK.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation update — the changes are explanatory prose and tables. Existing snippets do not need to be modified.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nBoth READMEs already contain all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The edits above only expand existing sections and add sub-sections within `Key concepts`.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\n2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\n3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n5. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirms no broken snippet placeholders.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Update Core and Responses READMEs for new logging and session-ID features (beta.22 / beta.2)"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-17T10:38:58.6747231Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6747657Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6747931Z **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary), `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` (secondary)
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6748391Z **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/`
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6748936Z **Triggered by:** commit 48e9f7bd852c033dbe6680c68a3197cb55722eca (PR #58274) by @RaviPidaparthi
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6749298Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6749377Z ## What Changed
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6749491Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6749682Z PR #58274 added two notable features that are unrepresented in the READMEs:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6749971Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6751519Z 1. **Core beta.22** — `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is now auto-registered inside `AgentHostBuilder` and logs every inbound HTTP request (method, path, status code, duration, correlation headers `x-request-id` / `x-ms-client-request-id`, OTel trace ID). Successful requests are logged at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6753063Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6754210Z 2. **Responses beta.2** — Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation via SHA-256 of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` (63-char lowercase hex); structured `Information`-level logs added to all endpoint handlers; new `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage call.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6755267Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6755343Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6755449Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6756633Z - The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` is a single sentence that says "Enable ASP.NET Core logging to diagnose startup issues" — it does not mention the auto-enabled `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`, what it logs, at which levels, or that query strings are excluded.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6758002Z - The `Key concepts` section of the Core README has no entry for the inbound request logging behaviour.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6759231Z - The `Logging` section in `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` does not document the new handler-level structured logs, the `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, or how to surface them.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6760384Z - The Responses README has no explanation of the `agent_session_id` field or how its value is determined (deterministic hash vs. random fallback).
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6760900Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6760969Z <details>
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6761274Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6761516Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6761826Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6762244Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6762325Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6762463Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6762680Z #### File 1 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6762950Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6763319Z **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~82) and replace it with an expanded description:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6763790Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6763856Z ```markdown
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6764020Z ### Logging
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6770918Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6771903Z `AgentHostBuilder` automatically enables structured HTTP request logging via `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. For every inbound request the middleware emits structured log entries under the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` category:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6772943Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6773034Z | Event | Level | Fields |
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6773252Z |-------|-------|--------|
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6773637Z | Request started | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `xRequestId`, `clientRequestId`, `traceId` |
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6774270Z | Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6774872Z | Request completed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | `method`, `path`, `statusCode`, `durationMs`, `traceId` |
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6775221Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6775437Z > **Security note:** Query strings are never logged to avoid capturing sensitive data.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6775760Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6776050Z To enable these logs, configure the minimum log level in your `appsettings.json` or host configuration:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6776439Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6776509Z ```json
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6776665Z {
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6776821Z   "Logging": {
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6776996Z     "LogLevel": {
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6777214Z       "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core": "Information"
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6777479Z     }
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6777623Z   }
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6777771Z }
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6778123Z ```
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6778209Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6778798Z The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6779392Z ```
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6779478Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6779885Z **Also add** a `### InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` entry to the `## Key concepts` section, after the existing `### Health endpoint` entry:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6780408Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6780485Z ```markdown
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6780662Z ### Inbound request logging
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6780802Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6782104Z `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically by `AgentHostBuilder`. It logs the method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) for every HTTP request. Successful requests are logged at `Information` level; 4xx/5xx responses at `Warning`. Query strings are intentionally excluded to avoid capturing sensitive data in logs.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6783569Z ```
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6783654Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6783946Z #### File 2 — `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6784236Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6784618Z **Edit** the `### Logging` subsection under `## Troubleshooting` (currently at line ~197) to replace the single-sentence placeholder:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6785083Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6785155Z ```markdown
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6785309Z ### Logging
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6785527Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6786112Z The library emits structured `Information`-level logs for all Responses API endpoint operations. Log category: `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses.Internal.ResponseEndpointHandler`.
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6786784Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6786872Z Key log events per endpoint:
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6787027Z 
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6787094Z - **POST /
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6787256Z ...
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6787462Z (truncated, showing first 65536 bytes of 88003 total)
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6787906Z ##[endgroup]
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6788231Z ##[group]tools.json (4727 bytes)
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6788444Z {
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6788717Z   "servers": {
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6788897Z     "github": [
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6789065Z       {
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6789236Z         "name": "get_commit",
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6789553Z         "description": "Get details for a commit from a GitHub repository"
2026-04-17T10:38:58.6789879Z       },
